# Changelog

All notable changes to `@dypai-ai/client-sdk` will be documented in this file.

## [1.12.0] - 2026-05-26

### Changed

- `dypai.api.upload()` now defaults `params.operation` to `"upload"`, so dedicated storage upload endpoints no longer require callers to pass `operation` explicitly. Callers can still override via `params` for legacy multiplex endpoints.

## [1.11.0] - 2026-05-17

### Added

- Added contract-first endpoint typings generated from DYPAI endpoint YAML contracts.
- Exported endpoint contract helper types for generated API maps.

### Changed

- Typed API clients now restrict endpoint names by HTTP method when a generated endpoint contract map is provided.
- Preserved the legacy endpoint typing shape for existing projects while marking it as deprecated.

## [1.10.7] - 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- `useChat` now treats any `Content-Type: text/event-stream` response as an SSE stream, even when browser CORS rules hide custom Vercel AI stream headers.

## [1.10.6] - 2026-05-12

### Fixed

- `useChat` now supports the Vercel AI SDK UI Message Stream protocol (`x-vercel-ai-ui-message-stream: v1`) used by DYPAI agent streaming endpoints.
- Preserved compatibility with legacy Vercel data streams and the previous DYPAI plain-text stream fallback.

### Changed

- `useChat` now keeps streamed tool calls and tool results in `message.parts` while still accumulating assistant text in `message.content`.

## [1.10.5] - 2026-05-10

### Fixed

- Smart Upload now keeps `confirm` and `client_upload` SDK-owned even if app params include those keys by mistake. The prepare request always sends `confirm:false` and the confirm request always sends `confirm:true`.

### Changed

- Clarified file upload docs: browser code should pass only domain params to `dypai.api.upload(...)`; `confirm` and `client_upload` are internal Smart Upload flags.

## [1.10.3] - 2026-05-07

### Added

- Added `client.roles` for application role management.
- Added `client.roles.list()` so apps can load real roles from the backend instead of hardcoding role names.
- Added role types: `Role`, `RolePermissions`, `RoleListResponse`, `CreateRoleRequest`, and `UpdateRoleRequest`.

## [1.10.2] - 2026-05-07

### Changed

- Password recovery now uses `redirects.passwordRecovery` as the default `redirectTo`, so apps do not need to pass it manually from every forgot-password page.
- Recovery requests include the configured public `x-api-key` header when present, matching the rest of the auth API calls.
- Recovery callbacks are replayed from durable browser state for late React subscribers, preventing reset pages from showing "link expired" before the SDK has finished initialization.
- `setPassword(password)` now supports both reset-token links and active recovery/invite sessions.
- Invalid or expired reset tokens clear stored recovery state and update `lastError`.
- React `useAuth()` now exposes `isPasswordRecovery`.

## [1.10.0] - 2026-04-10

### Added

- **Typed error hierarchy** (`src/errors.ts`): SDK now exposes a Supabase-style error class hierarchy so consumers can handle errors with `instanceof` / type guards instead of inspecting status codes.
  - `AuthSessionMissingError` — no active session when one is required
  - `AuthInvalidCredentialsError` — wrong email/password on sign in
  - `AuthInvalidTokenError` — JWT/reset/magic link token is invalid, expired, or revoked
  - `AuthWeakPasswordError` — password fails configured strength requirements (with `reasons[]`)
  - `AuthEmailNotVerifiedError` — operation requires a verified email
  - `AuthUserAlreadyExistsError` — sign-up with an existing email
  - `RetryableFetchError` — transient network/server error (status 0/408/429/5xx)
  - Type guards: `isDypaiError`, `isAuthSessionMissingError`, `isAuthInvalidCredentialsError`, `isAuthInvalidTokenError`, `isAuthWeakPasswordError`, `isAuthEmailNotVerifiedError`, `isAuthUserAlreadyExistsError`, `isRetryableFetchError`, `isRetryableStatus`
  - `classifyAuthError(message, status, code)` — internal helper that converts a generic auth error response into the most specific subclass

### Changed

- **`parseAuthError`** in `AuthModule` now returns a typed subclass instead of generic `DypaiError` when the error matches a known pattern (invalid credentials, expired token, weak password, etc.).
- **Internal `throw new DypaiError('No hay sesión activa', 401)`** replaced with `throw new AuthSessionMissingError(...)` across all auth methods (`getUser`, `getSession`, `updateUser`, `enableTwoFactor`, `disableTwoFactor`, `createOrganization`, `inviteMember`, `listOrganizations`).

## [1.9.0] - 2026-04-10

### Changed

- **Dual-token model**: `AuthModule` now stores both the **JWT** (for API calls, asymmetric EdDSA from better-auth's JWT plugin) and the **better-auth session token** (for auth operations like `get-session`, `refresh`, `sign-out`). API calls send the JWT in `Authorization`; auth operations use the session token. Fixes refresh-on-page-reload and prevents token mismatches.
- **401 handling (Supabase-style)**: API service no longer retries on 401. The auto-refresh ticker (every 30s) refreshes proactively before expiry. On 401, `onUnauthorized` clears the session and surfaces the error. Removes retry storms when multiple components fire simultaneously.
- **Session validation on init**: `_recoverSession` now validates the stored session against the server immediately, catching stale tokens (e.g. after auth system migration) instead of waiting for an API 401.
- **`setPassword` auto-login**: When the engine returns session tokens after a successful password reset, the SDK stores them so the user lands on the app already logged in.
- **`DypaiClient` redirect preservation**: Auto-redirect after password recovery now preserves the original query params (`?token=...&type=recovery`), fixing the case where the destination page received an empty URL.

### Added

- **BroadcastChannel for cross-tab sync**: Uses native `BroadcastChannel` (with storage event fallback) to instantly notify other tabs of `SIGNED_OUT` and `SESSION_UPDATED` events.
- **Smart retry on refresh errors**: Distinguishes terminal 4xx errors (clear session) from transient 5xx/408/429 errors (keep session, retry on next tick).
- **Clock skew tolerance**: 10-second margin in token expiration checks to tolerate desynced client clocks.
- **Storage versioning + migration**: Sessions are now stored with `version: 2`. Old v1 sessions (without `session_token`) are detected and cleaned automatically on the next page load.

### Removed

- **`onTokenExpired` callback**: No longer needed. Token refresh is proactive via the auto-refresh ticker, not reactive on 401. Use `onUnauthorized` to handle terminal auth failures.
- **`_isRetry` parameter on `callApi`**: Internal parameter removed alongside the retry logic.

### Migration notes

- **Existing user sessions will be invalidated once.** Tokens stored in localStorage from previous SDK versions (v1 format, HS256) are not compatible with the new asymmetric verification (EdDSA via JWKS). Users will be logged out automatically and need to sign in again to receive a v2 session.
- No application code changes are required. The public SDK API (`signIn`, `signUp`, `signOut`, `useAuth`, `onAuthStateChange`, etc.) is unchanged.

## [1.5.0] - 2026-04-09

### Changed

- **Authentication stack**: Client auth flows are aligned with the DYPAI **better-auth** backend (replacing the previous GoTrue-style integration). Session handling and token refresh remain compatible with `createClient()`; ensure your project’s auth API matches the current DYPAI control plane.

### Added

- **Auth (`AuthModule`)**: `signInWithOtp`, `signInWithMagicLink`, TOTP **2FA** (`enableTwoFactor`, `verifyTwoFactor`, `disableTwoFactor`), and **organizations** (`createOrganization`, `listOrganizations`, `inviteMember`).
- **React**: `useAuth` exposes the new auth and org helpers; **`useChat`** / **`useChatList`** for AI chat UIs (message streaming, sessions, tool calls).

### Dependencies

- `better-auth` added as a runtime dependency for typed client usage where applicable.

## [1.4.0] - 2026-04-03

### Added

- **Realtime**: WebSocket client aligned with the DYPAI engine (`/realtime?token=…`).
  - `DypaiClient.realtime` — `DypaiRealtimeClient` with Supabase-style `.channel()` / `.subscribe()` for `postgres_changes`, broadcast, and presence
  - React: `useRealtime`, `useChannel`, `useChannelMessages`, `useChannels` (shared single WebSocket via `realtimeShared`)
- Exported types: `DypaiRealtimeClient`, `RealtimeChannel`, `RealtimeEvent`, `SubscribeState`, and related channel/presence types

## [1.1.0] - 2026-03-27

### Added

- **Direct Database Access** (`client.db.direct`): CRUD and raw SQL without creating endpoints or workflows. Requires `serviceRoleKey` (admin-level).
  - `DirectDBModule` with `.from(table)` and `.sql(query, params)`
  - `DirectQueryBuilder` with chainable filters (`.eq()`, `.gt()`, `.in()`, `.like()`, `.isNull()`, etc.)
  - Terminal operations: `.select()`, `.insert()`, `.update()`, `.delete()`, `.upsert()`
  - Auto-chunking for bulk inserts over 1,000 rows
  - Safety: unfiltered UPDATE/DELETE blocked client-side
- New `serviceRoleKey` option in `createClient()` config
- Exported `DirectDBModule`, `DirectQueryBuilder`, `FilterCondition` types

## [1.0.0] - 2026-03-26

First stable release.

### Features

- **Authentication**: Sign in (email/password, OAuth, OTP/magic link), sign up, sign out, password recovery, session management, auto-refresh tokens, cross-tab sync via Web Locks API
- **Database CRUD**: Fluent query builder (`client.db.from('table').select()`)
- **Custom Endpoints**: Typed API client for calling DYPAI workflow endpoints (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)
- **File Upload/Download**: Smart Upload via presigned URLs with progress tracking
- **User Management**: Admin module for listing, creating, updating, and deleting users
- **React Integration**: `DypaiProvider`, `useAuth`, `useEndpoint`, `useAction`, `useUpload`, `ProtectedRoute`
- **TypeScript**: Full type safety with generic `createClient<Database, Api>()` for typed endpoints and database tables
- **Dual Build**: ESM + CJS outputs with TypeScript declarations
- **Auto-generated Docs**: TypeDoc integration with CI deploy to GitHub Pages

### Testing

- 292 unit tests across 8 test suites
- 88% statement coverage, 75% branch coverage

### Breaking Changes (from 0.0.x)

- `StorageModule` removed — use `client.api.upload()` / `client.api.download()` instead
- Old markdown docs removed — replaced by auto-generated TypeDoc at [dypai-solutions.github.io/client-sdk](https://dypai-solutions.github.io/client-sdk/)
- `prepublishOnly` no longer swaps README — full README is now published to npm
